description: Static analysis rubric for feature completeness: skill pipeline wiring, frontend-backend API connectivity, OPAR loop coverage, and infrastructure readiness. Complements the reference-data quality eval (criteria.json).

domains.name: skill_pipeline
domains.description: Are all skill directories wired through the dispatch layer, implemented with real logic, covered by output contracts, and reachable from OPAR intent classification?

dimensions.name: Skill Directory-to-Dispatch Parity
dimensions.description: Every skills/ subdirectory that contains a SKILL.md (excluding sector-packs/, references/, and _capability_matrix.json) should be registered in app/skills/dispatch.py via @register(). Unregistered skills are dead code — OPAR can never invoke them.

dimensions.name: Dispatch Handler Implementation Depth
dimensions.description: Each @register() handler in dispatch.py must be a real implementation (>5 non-blank lines in the handler body), not a stub or passthrough. Stubs silently return empty results and corrupt OPAR reflection.

dimensions.name: Skill Output Contract Coverage
dimensions.description: Each registered skill should have a corresponding Output dataclass in app/skills/contracts.py. Missing contracts mean the Act phase cannot validate LLM outputs, leading to silent data-quality failures downstream.

dimensions.name: OPAR Intent-to-Plan Mapping Coverage
dimensions.description: Every IntentClass value in app/opar/models.py must appear as a handled case in app/opar/plan.py. Unhandled intents fall through to generic_qa and lose all structured output.

domains.name: api_connectivity
domains.description: Do all frontend API calls resolve to backend routes? Are backend features exposed in the UI? Is the API versioning consistent? Are the new FP&A endpoints fully wired?

dimensions.name: Frontend Call Resolution Rate
dimensions.description: Every unique API path called from frontend .tsx/.ts files (via apiGet/apiPost/apiPut/apiUpload or href) must resolve to a registered backend route in app/routers/. Unresolved calls produce 404s at runtime.

dimensions.name: Backend Feature Frontend Exposure
dimensions.description: 8 backend feature groups (benchmarks, sector-packs, initiative milestones/actuals, consolidation, cost-to-serve, business-case exports, incremental analyze) have zero frontend pages calling them. Score = feature groups with at least one frontend call / 8.

dimensions.name: API Version Consistency
dimensions.description: Backend marks /api/ (unversioned) paths as deprecated (Sunset: 2027-01-01). Frontend should use /api/v1/ consistently. Score = calls using /api/v1/ prefix / total API calls. Mixed usage creates upgrade-path debt.

dimensions.name: FP&A Endpoint Wiring Completeness
dimensions.description: The three FP&A endpoints introduced in the March 2026 enhancements (GET /api/v1/trends/{id}, GET /api/v1/bva/{id}, GET /api/v1/payment-terms/{id}) must be present in both backend routers AND called from frontend pages. 1 point each side per endpoint (6 total → /6*10).
dimensions.scoring_method: sum(backend_present + frontend_present for each of 3 endpoints) / 6 * 10

domains.name: opar_loop
domains.description: Are all four OPAR phases implemented with substantive code? Does plan.py handle every intent? Does reflect.py have quality gates for core skill outputs?

dimensions.name: Phase Module Non-Empty Coverage
dimensions.description: All four OPAR phase modules (observe.py, plan.py, act.py, reflect.py) must exist under app/opar/ and contain >100 lines of code. Thin or absent phase files indicate incomplete loop implementation.
dimensions.scoring_method: 2.5 pts per phase file present with >100 lines (max 10)

dimensions.name: Intent Handler Coverage in Plan
dimensions.description: plan.py must contain a handling branch (as string match) for every IntentClass value defined in models.py. Unhandled intents silently fall back to generic_qa, bypassing all structured analysis.

dimensions.name: Quality Gate Coverage in Reflect
dimensions.description: reflect.py must reference quality-check logic for core skill output types: validate_core_skill_outputs (generic gate), savings-related validation, and peer_benchmarker output validation. Missing gates allow corrupt skill outputs to reach the final response.

domains.name: infrastructure
domains.description: Are connectors implemented with required methods? Are health endpoints present? Are LLM-facing endpoints rate-limited?

dimensions.name: Connector Method Completeness
dimensions.description: Each of the 6 concrete connectors (ariba_csv, bank_mt940, gst_portal, hrms_csv, sap_odata, tally_xml) must define at least one of: ingest(), fetch(), authenticate(), or connect(). Connectors missing these entry points cannot be called by ingestion.py.

dimensions.name: Health Endpoint Coverage
dimensions.description: app/main.py must expose GET /health (liveness) and GET /health/ready (readiness — returns 503 if Anthropic key missing). Both are required for Kubernetes probes and deployment automation.

dimensions.name: Rate Limit Coverage for LLM Endpoints
dimensions.description: The global slowapi Limiter must be configured in main.py. Chat endpoints (which call Anthropic API) should have per-route @limiter.limit() decorators distinct from the default. Missing per-route limits allow LLM cost spikes.
